headlines.title: President Trump Signs Executive Order Imposing 100% Tariffs on Some Patented Drugs
headlines.summary: President Trump signed an executive order targeting pharmaceutical companies without 'most favored nation' pricing deals, imposing tariffs up to 100% on select patented drugs. US drug buyers and global pharma firms face higher costs, prompting negotiations; this escalates trade pressures amid healthcare pricing debates.

headlines.title: Defense Secretary Hegseth Asks Army Chief Gen. Randy George to Step Down
headlines.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth requested early retirement for Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George amid US war against Iran. Pentagon leadership faces shakeup, affecting military readiness; a successor search begins as operations continue.

headlines.title: US Strikes Destroy Highway Bridge and Health Site Near Tehran
headlines.summary: US strikes targeted a major highway bridge outside Tehran and a public health institution after President Trump's threats. Iranian infrastructure suffers setbacks, disrupting transport and services for millions; further targets like power plants loom.

headlines.title: Myanmar Junta Chief Min Aung Hlaing Wins Parliamentary Vote for Presidency
headlines.summary: Junta leader Min Aung Hlaing secured the presidency in a pro-military parliament vote, formalizing control five years post-coup. Myanmar's 50 million face entrenched military rule amid civil war; international condemnation grows as sham election cements power.

framings.angle: Broadcast vote count showing comfortable threshold passage
Highlighted recent lopsided election won by army-backed party
Recalled 2021 coup against Aung San Suu Kyi sparking resistance
Noted majority threshold met by coup leader in parliament

headlines.title: NATO Launches First Coordinated Response to Suspected Baltic Sea Cable Sabotage
headlines.summary: NATO responds to severed deep-sea cable in Baltic Sea amid suspected sabotage campaign against infrastructure. Alliance members enhance defenses, affecting transatlantic data flows; investigations pinpoint culprits amid hybrid threat fears.

headlines.title: Human Rights Watch Reports Burkina Faso Army Causes Most Civilian Deaths
headlines.summary: HRW finds Burkina Faso security forces and militias behind most civilian killings, targeting Fulani ethnic group more brutally than militants.  Thousands affected in Sahel conflict; calls for accountability intensify global scrutiny on juntas.

framings.angle: Cited HRW on army and militias exceeding militant violence levels
Emphasized Fulani targeting by brutal security forces per HRW

headlines.title: President Trump Removes US Attorney General Pam Bondi from Office
headlines.summary: Trump dismissed Attorney General Pam Bondi, whose tenure focused on Epstein files handling. Justice Department leadership changes amid political controversies; acting AG appointed as confirmation process starts for replacement.

headlines.title: NASA and SpaceX Launch Crew-10 to Retrieve Stuck ISS Astronauts
headlines.summary: SpaceX Falcon 9 carried NASA astronauts Anne McClain and Nichole Ayers, Japan's Takuya Onishi, Russia's Kirill Peskov to ISS. Butch Wilmore and Suni Williams, stuck nine months, prepare return; ensures station operations continuity.

headlines.title: Artemis II Astronauts Ignite Engines for Lunar Flyby Trajectory
headlines.previousContext: Crew fired engines Thursday, departing Earth orbit toward Moon for first crewed flyby since 1972
headlines.summary: NASA's Artemis II crew of three Americans and one Canadian ignited engines, heading for lunar flyby, solar eclipse view, and distance records. Paves lunar return path despite orbital hurdles; return expected weeks later.

headlines.title: UN Security Council Schedules Vote on Resolution for Hormuz Defensive Force
headlines.previousContext: Bahraini proposal for defensive means to reopen Strait gains US backing amid disruptions
headlines.summary: UNSC votes Friday on Gulf-led resolution authorizing defensive force to secure Strait of Hormuz shipping. Global energy trade for millions at risk from Iranian actions; approval could deploy naval protections soon.

framings.angle: Covered Trump warnings on targeting Iranian bridges and power plants
Framed amid tensions over energy supplies and economic stability liveblog
